title: [BUG] duplicateValues conditional formatting rule is not maintained and breaks saving
author: jeffsenn
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

If a conditional formatting rule of type "duplicateValue" exists in an Excel file, simply reading and writing back the excel sheet using exceljs will fail.  Well mostly fail.  Excel will throw an alert dialog with a message like this: "We found a problem with some content in 'xBook1.xlsx'. Do you want us to try to recover as much as we can? If you trust the source of this workbook, click Yes."

Usually the problem will be repaired, but in that case the conditional formatting is lost.

The XML embedded looks like this before:

```
<conditionalFormatting sqref="A1:A1048576"><cfRule type="duplicateValues" dxfId="0" priority="1"/></conditionalFormatting>
```

And after:

```
<conditionalFormatting sqref="A1:A1048576"/>
```

Lib version: 4.3.0

## Steps To Reproduce

Create an excel.  Add any conditional formatting "duplicateValues" rule.
Use code like this:

```javascript 
const workbook = new Excel.Workbook();
await workbook.xlsx.readFile(filename, {});
await workbook.xlsx.writeFile('output-'+filename);
```

Try to open the output file in Excel.

Examples attached

## The expected behaviour:

Excel does not have an alert.  Conditional formatting is retained.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

I don't have expertise to suggest an answer.  Even if rules are not internally supported it would be good to maintain them for input/output.

[before.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/10223776/before.xlsx)
[after.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/10223778/after.xlsx)

comments.author: jeffsenn
comments.body: I suppose this is related to #1421
